source: BERDE, LEED v4.1, RA 7586 (NIPAS Act), DENR Guidelines

rules.description: Avoid development on prime agricultural land (CARP/CARL protected)
rules.notes: RA 6657 (CARP) protects irrigated farmland. DAR reclassification required. BERDE penalizes greenfield development on productive agricultural land.

rules.description: Avoid development within wetlands, mangroves, or NIPAS areas
rules.notes: RA 7586 (NIPAS Act) and RA 7161 prohibit construction in protected areas. EIA required for projects near protected zones.

rules.description: Brownfield or infill sites preferred over greenfield
rules.notes: BERDE awards 2 credits for brownfield remediation. Reduces sprawl, preserves natural land, and utilizes existing infrastructure.

rules.description: Proximity to public transportation — maximum distance to transit stop
rules.notes: 400m ≈ 5 minute walk to jeepney/bus stop, MRT/LRT station, or PUV terminal. BERDE and LEED award credits. Reduces car dependency and associated emissions.

rules.description: Minimum solar reflectance index (SRI) for roofing
rules.notes: Low-slope roof SRI ≥ 78 (LEED). Steep-slope ≥ 29. Alternatively, use vegetated (green) roof for 50%+ of roof area. White TPO/PVC membranes typically SRI 80+.

rules.description: Minimum percentage of site hardscape that is shaded, permeable, or high-SRI
rules.notes: Dark asphalt: SRI 0. Light concrete: SRI 35. Permeable pavers, grass pavers, or tree canopy shading all qualify. Reduces ambient temperatures by 2-5°C.
Extensive green roof (sedum, grasses, 75-150mm substrate) reduces roof surface temp by 30-40°C, extends roof membrane life 2-3×, provides stormwater retention.

rules.description: Existing mature trees must be preserved or transplanted
rules.notes: DENR requires cutting permits for trees >200mm diameter. Preservation preferred. If removal unavoidable, plant replacement at 1:10 ratio per DENR DAO 2004-44.

rules.description: Native species minimum percentage in new planting
rules.notes: Native plants support local pollinators, require less irrigation, and resist local pests. BERDE awards credits for exceeding 60%.

rules.description: Light pollution reduction — exterior lighting must not trespass property boundaries
rules.notes: All exterior lighting must be full-cutoff fixtures (no uplight). Reduces energy waste, protects nocturnal wildlife, and respects neighbors. BERDE prerequisite.

rules.description: Pesticide and herbicide management plan for landscaping
rules.notes: Integrated Pest Management (IPM) prioritizes non-chemical controls. BERDE prohibits neonicotinoid pesticides. Organic fertilizers preferred for food-producing landscapes.

rules.description: Bicycle parking minimum for commercial buildings
rules.notes: 5% of total vehicle parking count. BERDE and LEED requirement. Provide covered, secure bike racks. Shower/changing facilities for 0.5% of occupants.
Designated close-to-entrance spots for EVs, hybrids, or carpools. EV charging infrastructure for at least 2% of spaces.

rules.description: EV charging station readiness for new parking structures
rules.notes: At minimum, 10% of parking spaces should have electrical conduit roughed in for future EV charger installation. EVSE-ready per DOE-PH emerging guidelines.
